title: Stallari Catalog Entry
description: Schema for entries in the registry catalog (plugins/*.json). Supports both plugin and pack types via discriminated union on the 'type' field.

then.description: DD-265 belt-and-braces: library form factor requires an SPM dependency in stallari-harness/Package.swift — only first-party builds can produce library blades.

type.description: Entry type. Plugins provide MCP tools; packs are workflow manifests.

license.description: SPDX license identifier. Required for plugins.

contract.description: Domain contract identifier(s). String for single contract; array for multi-contract plugins (e.g. cloudflare-blade declares four). Required for plugins.

risk_class.description: DD-280 coarse risk class for this plugin's tool surface.

tools.description: DD-333 per-tool catalog inventory + granularity declarations. Per-tool granularity is REQUIRED unless the tool is listed in non_conformance_rationale.affected_tools (DD-333 F.1, pack-spec 4.2.0). build-catalog.js enforces the cross-field constraint procedurally with actionable error output (JSON Schema 2020-12 cannot express cross-field invariants). Pairs with S-MCP-001 `.error` lint in stallari-secops-scanner. Per-tool, not per-blade: different tools in the same pack legitimately have different granularity — a blade with `list_notes` (server-side) + `dump_all` (no scope) declares each honestly.

name.description: MCP tool name as registered by the blade at list_tools time.

risk_class.description: DD-280 risk class per tool. Per-tool declaration takes precedence over the plugin-level risk_class for tools that diverge from the blade's typical posture.

granularity.description: DD-333 four-dimension granularity declaration per tool. Required at pack-spec 4.0.0 (Phase D cutover 2026-05-21) UNLESS the tool is listed in non_conformance_rationale.affected_tools (DD-333 F.1). Declare honestly — `client-side`/`none`/`unstable`/`minimal` is the correct declaration when that's the actual behaviour. The assembler's verdict logic refuses evidentiary-level packets through inferred-worst-case tools; honest under-declaration passes conformance.

scope_filtering.description: server-side: tool accepts a scope argument and the plugin honours it before bytes leave the plugin's address space (safe for over-fetch threat model). client-side: tool returns unfiltered results and the assembler filters post-hoc (carries over-fetch leak). none: tool has no concept of scope (data is naturally unscoped, or the plugin punts entirely). non-conforming-explicit (DD-333 F.1): computed at build time in build-catalog.js for tools listed in non_conformance_rationale.affected_tools whose own granularity block is omitted. MUST NOT be hand-written by authors — authors declare non_conformance_rationale at top level and leave granularity off on listed tools.

field_projection.description: per-field: tool accepts a fields=[...] argument and returns only requested fields. top-level: tool returns a fixed projection (assembler must select the right tool variant, e.g. get_summary vs get_full). none: tool returns its native shape regardless of caller intent.

deterministic_ordering.description: stable: identical inputs produce identical output order (content_hash reproducible). unstable: output order varies between calls (hash will not reproduce). unsorted: tool explicitly returns an unordered set (assembler must sort to derive a hash).

audit_surface.description: structured: tool returns a _meta envelope alongside results (matched_total, returned, filtered_by, redactions) the assembler lifts into ContextPacket.provenance. minimal: tool returns only the data payload; assembler records call shape only. none: tool returns opaque data with no introspection.

domain_scope.description: DD-333 F.4 — 5th granularity dimension declaring whether this tool's result-set originates from a single user-declared domain or from multiple/all domains (cross-DD with DD-341 domain substrate and DD-338 _meta.domain_attribution). single: tool's response covers exactly ONE user-declared domain — either the tool is scoped by upstream binding (e.g. a per-account blade) or the caller's domain selection is honoured server-side. multi: tool's response may legitimately span multiple domains in one call (e.g. cross-account search, cross-domain aggregation) — at A.2.dom the blade must populate _meta.domain_attribution; assembler partitions per-domain on packet ingest. non-conforming-explicit: build-catalog.js derives this for tools listed in non_conformance_rationale.domain_scope_unspecified — author has explicitly acknowledged the tool cannot yet declare domain_scope; assembler treats as worst-case (single-only safe). Optional at F.4 ship; promoted to required at F.4.b after A.2.dom blade backfill. S-DOM-002 lint (severity warning at F.4) flags tools declaring `single` that also expose a user-domain-shaped scope argument — likely indicates the author meant `multi` (scope arg admits multiple domains).

non_conformance_rationale.description: DD-333 F.1 (pack-spec 4.2.0) — explicit non-conformance declaration. When a plugin's tools[] entry lacks a `granularity:` block AND the tool name appears in `affected_tools` here, the plugin is accepted-with-rationale rather than rejected. build-catalog.js derives `granularity.scope_filtering: "non-conforming-explicit"` (plus worst-case row on the other three dimensions) for each listed tool. Downstream UI ([[DD-189]] amendment), memory ([[DD-301]] amendment), and assembler verdict ([[DD-333]] F.2) consume the per-tool non-conformance flag. See stallari-pack-spec/docs/non-conformance-rationale.md.

reason.description: Non-empty author-supplied explanation of why this pack/tool cannot meet the granularity contract. Surfaces in marketplace UI ([[DD-189]] amendment) and audit Tracks ([[DD-307]]).

scope_filtering_off.description: Explicit acknowledgment. MUST be true when the block is present (the entire block is omitted on the conforming path). const:true rather than just `true` so AJV emits a clear error if the value is false.

contamination_risks.description: Controlled vocabulary. memory-source-tainted: output flows into DD-301 memory without scope-filtering provenance. audit-context-leak: lacks _meta envelope disclosure; DD-307 audit trail blind. cross-scope-packet-bleed: unfiltered results that the assembler post-hoc filters; restricted bytes cross the plugin/assembler boundary. other-see-reason: escape hatch; the `reason` field MUST elaborate.

affected_tools.description: Tool names from this entry's tools[].name. Every entry here MUST cross-reference an actual tool name (procedural gate in build-catalog.js + S-MCP-002 in stallari-secops-scanner enforce the cross-field invariant — JSON Schema 2020-12 cannot express it).

domain_scope_unspecified.description: DD-333 F.4 — additive non-conformance escape hatch for the 5th granularity dimension (domain_scope). Optional list of tool names from this entry's tools[].name that legitimately cannot yet declare domain_scope (upstream provider doesn't expose per-domain partitioning, blade pre-dates DD-341 substrate, etc.). build-catalog.js derives `granularity.domain_scope: "non-conforming-explicit"` for each listed tool (sister to existing scope_filtering derivation). Cross-field invariant (every entry must cross-reference an actual tools[].name) is enforced procedurally in build-catalog.js. Promoted to mandatory backfill at F.4.b after A.2.dom blade declarations land.

runtime.description: Runtime kind. 'library' (DD-265) means the plugin is compiled into the Stallari daemon binary via SPM — never fetched, never installed; the consumer is the daemon process itself.

bundled_in.description: DD-265: name of the Stallari binary that compiles this library blade in (e.g. 'stallari-harness'). Set only when runtime == 'library'.

then.description: DD-265: library-runtime entries MUST NOT carry binary or package — the consumer is the daemon binary itself, not a separately-fetched artefact.

consent_tile_link.description: DD-265: deep-link (e.g. 'stallari://settings/general/local-corpus/mail') opened by the marketplace Manage button when runtime == 'library'. Optional — absence means render a passive Bundled tile with no Manage button (vault form factor).

author_type.description: Authorship class. DD-265: required for runtime == 'library' (must be 'first-party' — only first-party builds can edit Package.swift). Legacy values 'stallari' (≡ first-party) and 'third-party' (≡ community) remain accepted.

hidden.description: When true, the entry is excluded from the marketplace catalog build.

services.description: Abstract service names this entry provides or uses. Array of strings is the canonical shape; object form is legacy (apple-docs-mcp et al.).

pack_spec.description: Pack spec version (e.g. "1.0"). Present only for type=pack.

skill_count.description: Number of skills in this pack. Present only for type=pack.

conformance.description: Contract conformance test results. Primarily for plugins.

inference.description: Optional inference declaration for entries that embed LLM calls.

setup_complexity.description: Setup complexity from plugin setup.complexity. 'none' = no credentials needed. Null = not declared.

auth_model.description: Primary authentication model from plugin setup metadata.

credential_count.description: Number of credential fields required. 0 = no setup needed.
